equipment_condition_basis.abnormal_ie_adjustment_source: ETAP Arc Flash Analysis App Note 2020 + EasyPower technical bulletin TB-AF-2019 (industry consensus 1.2–1.5× range; NFPA 70E §130.5(A) does NOT prescribe — engineer must validate against site assessment)

method_fallback_trail.reason: theoretical formula always available; V_nom 400V within 50-15000V range

label.label_required_per: NFPA 70E:2024 §130.5(H) — metal-clad LV switchgear ≥240V (UK: voluntary best practice per HSG48)

equipment_condition.ie_adjustment_source: default (normal equipment — no adjustment per NFPA 70E §130.5(A))

label.label_required_per: NFPA 70E:2024 §130.5(H) — LV panelboard ≥240V (UK: voluntary best practice)

equipment_condition.ie_adjustment_source: default (normal equipment — no adjustment per NFPA 70E §130.5(A))

non_compliance_flags.message: Both cascade nodes fell back to Lee 1982 (conservative upper bound). Transcribe IEEE 1584:2018 Table 4 coefficients for VCB 600V class to get realistic IE values.

assumptions: fault-level intent consumed; per-node Ibf + X/R from it
db-layout-rollup intent consumed; equipment_type + ocpd_type from it; electrode_config auto-inferred to VCB for both nodes
t_clear_s engineer-declared (0.3s for ACB at MSB; 0.2s for MCCB at DB)
IE values are Lee 1982 conservative upper bounds — actual IE per IEEE 1584:2018 will be 2-5× lower when coefficients are transcribed
tool_call_pending: true on both nodes — calc.arc_flash_incident_energy contract shipped but runtime tool not yet implemented

rationale.chat_summary: UK 400V TPN commercial: 2-node cascade (MSB → DB-L1). IEEE 1584:2018 coefficients pending-verification → fell back to Lee 1982 theoretical (conservative upper bound). MSB IE ≈ 9.8 cal/cm² → Cat 3; DB-L1 IE ≈ 5.2 → Cat 2. Both nodes auto-inferred to VCB. Shock-approach distances from Table 130.4(C)(a) 151V-750V row. Labels recommended (UK best practice). tool_call_pending across cascade.

sections.title: Input Ingestion
sections.summary: Both intents consumed; engineer declared t_clear per node.

sections.title: Cascade Topology
sections.summary: 2-node depth-2 cascade: service_entrance → sub_feeder. All AC.

sections.title: Jurisdictional Framing
sections.summary: GB — voluntary best practice per HSG48 + IET CoP. Labels recommended, not statutory.

decisions.label: GB jurisdiction applied
decisions.rule: HSG48 + IET Code of Practice for In-Service Inspection

sections.title: Method Selection Summary
sections.summary: Both nodes fell back through IEEE 1584:2018 (skipped — coefficients pending) → IEEE 1584:2002 (skipped — pending) → Lee 1982 (applied). LEE_1982_FALLBACK_USED flag emitted info severity.

decisions.label: Lee 1982 fallback both nodes
decisions.summary: IEEE 1584:2018 + 2002 coefficients null. Lee theoretical applied as conservative upper bound.
decisions.code_clause: Lee 1982 (cited by IEEE 1584:2018 §2 + IEEE 1584:2002 Annex B)

sections.title: Per-node Arc-Flash Results
sections.summary: MSB: IE 9.8 → Cat 3 / AFB 1650mm. DB-L1: IE 5.2 → Cat 2 / AFB 1050mm.

sections.title: Shock-Approach Boundaries
sections.summary: Both nodes: 400V → Table 130.4(C)(a) row '151V to 750V'. Limited movable 3050mm, fixed 1070mm; restricted 305mm.

sections.title: Label Recommendations
sections.summary: Both nodes label_recommended: true. UK regulatory context: HSG48 + IET CoP best practice (voluntary, not statutory like US NFPA 70E §130.5(H)).

sections.title: Compliance + Assumptions + Tool-call Status
sections.summary: Compliant. Lee fallback flagged info-severity. tool_call_pending on both nodes. IE will be re-computed (lower) when IEEE 1584:2018 coefficients transcribed.

invariants.evidence: node_id path pattern: MSB-1 (root) and MSB-1.DB-L1 (sub-DB) both match ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9.\-]{0,63}$ and the second resolves parent_node_id='MSB-1' which exists in the cascade.

method_applied 'ieee_1584_2018' is in the controlled vocabulary {ieee_1584_2018, lee_1982, doan_dc, pending}; applies cleanly per IEEE 1584:2018 §4 for 400 V AC equipment within the standard's voltage/X-over-R range.

Numeric outputs gated by method_applied: incident_energy_cal_per_cm2 and arc_flash_boundary_mm are populated only because IEEE 1584:2018 fired (not 'pending'); PPE category derived from incident_energy per NFPA 70E Table 130.5(G).

provenance.provenance_note: LV (400 V) example uses Lee 1982 fallback because IEEE Std 1584-2018 600 V coefficient table at shared/standards/electrical/IEEE1584/method-2018-tables-1-3-600V-coefficients.json carries _status: pending-transcription-from-IEEE-1584-2018-tables-1-3 (IEEE Xplore paywall blocks verbatim transcription per Sprint A.3 source-vetting). Lee is the >15 kV theoretical-max model — over-predicts at 400 V; PPE category conservative-wrong (over-specs). Downstream label consumers MUST mark is_provisional=true + DRAFT marker per Sprint A.2 C2 convention. Resolves DEFECT_REGISTER C3 honesty gap raised by Reviewer 1's re-test.
